description_long: This is a robust, rectangular brick pumping station with a flat roof and a fortified appearance, featuring a series of evenly spaced circular and vertical slit windows on all sides. The structure is built from reddish-brown bricks, with a prominent stone base and a set of concrete steps leading to a single main entrance on one side. The upper section of the building is adorned with a row of decorative stone corbels and circular openings just below the roofline, suggesting ventilation or architectural detailing. The windows are narrow and tall, likely for security and structural reasons, and the building sits on a wide, paved concrete platform. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation aside from the corbelled roofline and the symmetrical arrangement of windows and openings. The structure appears solid and immovable, suitable for housing critical urban infrastructure.
description: A solid, rectangular brick pumping station with narrow windows, a flat roof, and a concrete base, featuring a main entrance with steps and decorative stonework near the roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with steps, three tall slit windows, and circular openings near the roof.
description_view_1: Side view with no entrance, displaying three tall slit windows and a lower rectangular window, plus the continuation of the decorative roofline.
description_view_2: Rear view with three tall slit windows and two small rectangular windows at the base, no entrance visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with three tall slit windows and two small rectangular windows at the base, similar to view 2 but with a wider paved area.
